text.title: Use Case Modeling Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: Authenticated Patient books an appointment.

section.title: Use case
section.code: id: uc-book-appointment trace_id: PROD-432 primary_actor: Authenticated Patient secondary_actors: - Clinic Scheduling Service - SMS Gateway goal: Book a clinician appointment for a specific date and time. preconditions: - "session.authenticated == true" - "patient.has_active_insurance == true" - "patient.verified_email == true" postconditions: - "appointment.status == 'booked'" - "sms_sent == true OR sms_queued == true" - "calendar.held_slot == appointment.slot_id" main_success_scenario: - 1. Patient selects clinician + available slot - 2. System reserves slot in Scheduling Service - 3. System persists appointment row + dispatches SMS via Gateway - 4. System returns booking_id and ICS link to patient alternative_flows: - id: alt-1 trigger: "slot becomes unavailable between selection and reservation" steps: - 1. Scheduling Service returns 409 on reserve - 2. System fetches next 3 nearest slots - 3. System prompts patient to pick one exception_flows: - id: exc-1 trigger: "SMS Gateway timeout" steps: - 1. System persists booking - 2. System queues SMS retry (3 attempts, exp backoff) - 3. System surfaces in-app notification as fallback - id: exc-2 trigger: "Scheduling Service 5xx" steps: - 1. System rolls back appointment row - 2. System surfaces "try again in 60s" to patient

section.title: Outcome
section.p: QA derives 6 test cases (1 main, 1 alt, 2 exception, 2 negative preconditions). AC team uses preconditions as Given clauses and exception triggers as Then clauses.
